bead.title: execute-bead: treat 'tracker lock timeout' as transient git contention (currently misclassifies concurrent-worker lock race as operator_attention and exits)
bead.description:
PROBLEM: When two or more `ddx work` workers run concurrently against the
same project and worker B reaches `FinalizeDurableAudit` at the same moment
that worker A holds the tracker lock, worker B times out, and the loop
classifies the timeout as `operator_attention` and exits — even though
nothing requires operator action. The other worker just needs to finish.

OBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain with two concurrent workers:
- Worker1 (pid 424293) was committing tracker state for niflheim-996afcc8.
- Worker2 (pid 681801), simultaneously finalizing niflheim-e43f04bb (an
  ADR-051 cold-tier PUT concurrency implementation that had already run to
  completion — 66091 tokens, 32m8.5s), hit `tracker lock timeout (max
  elapsed, lock: /home/erik/Projects/niflheim/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock,
  owner pid: 424293)` while trying to commit durable audit outputs.
- Worker2 exited with `operator attention required`, releasing
  niflheim-e43f04bb back to `open` without committing the audit trail,
  and leaving these paths dirty: `.ddx/beads.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`, `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl`,
  `.ddx/attachments`.

The work was successful — 32 minutes of agent time produced a clean
implementation and commit. The lock race nuked the audit attribution and
forced the worker to stop. Re-running it would either repeat the agent run
(burning more tokens) or hit `already_satisfied` (with the worktree-leak
documented in `ddx-274943fc`).

ROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1309`
(`finalizeDurableAuditOrStop`) calls `runtime.FinalizeDurableAudit` and
branches on `isTransientGitContention(err.Error())` to decide retry vs
operator-attention. The matcher at
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1019-1034` covers `.git/index.lock`
(via `gitlock.IsIndexLockError`) and the strings "unable to write new index
file", "cannot lock ref", "unable to update the ref" — but **does not cover
the DDx-specific tracker-lock error format** emitted by
`cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:243`:

  "tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)"

The tracker lock is a DDx-internal ordering lock around tracker writes
(`.ddx/.git-tracker.lock`), held briefly during tracker-state mutations.
Concurrent worker contention on this lock is structurally identical to
the `.git/index.lock` case the existing matcher handles, and the comment
on `isTransientGitContention` explicitly states the design intent:

  "A persistent cause (e.g. ENOSPC) simply keeps releasing+retrying the
   bead, which is still preferable to halting the whole unattended drain."

PROPOSED FIX: Extend `isTransientGitContention` to recognise the DDx
tracker-lock-timeout string class. Recommended implementation:

  1. Export a stable marker constant (or recogniser) from
     `cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go` rather than string-matching
     across packages. Suggested: a sentinel error type
     `TrackerLockTimeoutError` wrapping the existing fields (lockDir,
     ownerPID), with an `Is(err)` helper.
  2. Update `FinalizeDurableAudit` and its callers to wrap the timeout
     error with this sentinel, so callers can use `errors.Is` instead of
     substring matching.
  3. In `isTransientGitContention` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1019`, add a
     branch that returns true for `errors.Is(err, TrackerLockTimeoutErr)`.
     Keep the existing substring fallbacks for defense in depth.
  4. Confirm via test that worker2 in the observed scenario survives the
     lock timeout, re-enters the loop, and successfully commits on a
     subsequent iteration when the lock releases.

NON-SCOPE:
- Changing the tracker-lock retry policy itself (timeout duration, backoff
  curve) — only changing how the loop classifies the lock-timeout outcome.
- Changing concurrent-worker safety guarantees on tracker writes.
- Adding queue-level fairness or worker coordination (out of scope for
  this bead).
- Fixing the orphan-worktree leak that compounds this issue — tracked
  separately in `ddx-274943fc`.

IN-SCOPE FILES:
- cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go (export sentinel error type)
- cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go (wrap timeout with sentinel where
  it bubbles up — verify by reading line 41 and 127)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (extend `isTransientGitContention`)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go (new test for tracker-lock
  classification)

OUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:
- gitlock package (already correct for .git/index.lock; do not modify)
- cli/cmd/work.go (exit-reason rendering unchanged)
- Any other isTransientGitContention call sites — only the durable-audit
  branch matters for this bug.
bead.acceptance:
1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestIsTransientGitContention|TestFinalizeDurableAudit.*TrackerLock' -count=1` passes.
2. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for an error wrapping `TrackerLockTimeoutError`.
3. A unit test asserts `isTransientGitContention` returns true for the literal substring "tracker lock timeout" as a defense-in-depth fallback.
4. A unit test of `finalizeDurableAuditOrStop` injects a tracker-lock-timeout error and asserts: (a) `result.OperatorAttention` is NOT set, (b) the durable_audit_transient event is emitted, (c) the function returns false (don't stop).
5. `cd cli && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
